title: Judge Orders Kennedy Center to Make a Plan for Staying Open
summary: As the Trump administration seeks to move forward with renovations at the center, a judge has asked for its programming calendar.

title: Washington’s Reagan Airport Will Ground Flights for July 4 Events
summary: The runways at Ronald Reagan National Airport will be closed after noon on July 4, and for several hours on July 3. Other celebrations surrounding the nation’s 250th birthday are also expected to cause disruptions.

title: How Local Police Are Working for ICE, and an Unusual Data Center Dispute
summary: Plus, the gym is the new first date.

title: Trump Angrily Defends Iran Deal, Belittling the One Obama Signed
summary: President Trump denied that the United States would be part of a $300 billion rebuilding fund for Tehran and argued that his agreement was better than the one Barack Obama struck in 2015.

title: Bahrain Revoked Their Citizenship, and Then Tried to Expel Them to Iran
summary: Amid the war with Iran, Bahrain has stripped 69 people of their citizenship, including children, accusing them of disloyalty and rendering them stateless.

title: Mental Health Can Complicate Family Planning
summary: Five stories show the hurdles that people with psychological diagnoses face when considering parenthood.

title: The U.S. Economy Is Leaving Small Businesses Behind
summary: Small businesses say relentless pressures from tariffs and higher energy prices have sapped their resilience and finances.

title: What to Watch at the Federal Reserve’s June Meeting
summary: The U.S. central bank is widely expected to hold interest rates steady at Kevin M. Warsh’s first meeting as chairman.

title: Warsh Faces Tenuous Balancing Act in First Fed Meeting as Chairman
summary: Elevated inflation and the specter of rate increases will greet Kevin M. Warsh in his debut as chairman of the Federal Reserve.

title: Matthew Rhys, of ‘Widow’s Bay,’ Is on a Boat. Barely.
summary: The star of this seafaring horror-comedy, which wraps up Season 1 on Wednesday, also spends his downtime on the water.

title: This Senator Is an Internet Sensation. Is He Running for President?
summary: The 39-year-old senator has become an internet sensation for Democrats seeking a 2028 contender. He says he’s focused on winning a second term in November.

title: Takeaways From the Runoff and Primary Elections in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
summary: Lt. Gov. Burt Jones lost the Republican runoff for governor to the health care executive Rick Jackson despite the president’s endorsement. Mr. Trump’s picks won in other races.

title: Elon Musk’s Feud With Delaware May Transform Corporate America
summary: An angry Musk has taken his business elsewhere, and has urged other companies to follow suit.

title: Say Hello to the Newest N.Y.C. Area Code: 465
summary: There are millions of possible phone numbers under each area code, but about once a decade, this growing city runs out of digits.

title: Illinois Town Loses a Prison, Leaving Residents Worried About Jobs
summary: Lincoln, Ill., where Abraham Lincoln once worked in the local courthouse, has lost two colleges, a bottle factory and now a state prison.

title: As Mourning Begins, Deadly B-52 Crash Highlights Age of Bomber Fleet
summary: Investigators are just beginning to determine what went wrong, but the Air Force has struggled to maintain the planes, which were first introduced in 1955.

title: Beijing’s New Message to Its Citizens: Your Money Belongs at Home
summary: Eager to keep capital within its borders, China is restricting the ways individuals can engage with global markets.

title: ‘Backrooms’ and ‘Obsession’ Disrupt the 2026 Summer Box Office
summary: “Backrooms” and “Obsession,” two breakout horror films from first-time directors in their 20s, have arguably redefined what a summer blockbuster can be.

title: Dear A.I. Companies: The Doom Trolling Needs to Stop
summary: The major A.I. companies keep telling us how dangerous their new models can be, yet they must keep on building them.

title: The Real Reason Trump Never Stops Talking About Voter Fraud
summary: “Voter fraud” is not about fraud. It is about who votes and how.

title: What Could Happen in Iran?
summary: Three Times reporters examine the possibilities.

title: Late Night Wades Into the Reflecting Pool’s Algae Bloom
summary: President Trump “promised he would drain the swamp,” Jimmy Kimmel said. “Instead, he spent 14 million of our dollars building a new one.”

title: Private Jet Crashes on Highway in Laredo, Texas, Killing 1
summary: The plane’s crew reported mechanical problems and low fuel as they flew to Austin, Texas, from Mexico. Five of the six people on board survived the crash in Laredo.
